slides.title: Why Agentic AI?
slides.raw_text:
Why Agentic AI?
LLMs evolving from passive tools to active agents
●
Autonomous task orchestration
●
Need for memory, reasoning, planning
●

slides.title: AI Agents vs Agentic AI
slides.raw_text:
AI Agents vs Agentic AI
An AI Agent is an individual intelligent system that:
● Perceives → Decides → Acts to achieve a goal.
● It usually works alone and focuses on completing specific tasks.
Agentic AI is a system-level paradigm where:
● Multiple intelligent agents collaborate, plan, learn, and self-improve to achieve complex goals.
● It is about building ecosystems of agents, not just one.

slides.title: Reference Platform Architecture
slides.raw_text:
Reference Platform Architecture
○ Planner: Decomposes goals into actions or chains-of-thought. Can be
rule-based or LLM-based (chain-of-thought prompts). Guides overall strategy
○ Memory: Often split into working memory (short-term conversation context)
and persistent memory (long-term vector store)
○ Execution/Tools: Agents execute plans by calling external tools/APIs (e.g.,
search, databases, web APIs)
○ Observability: Integrate logging, tracing, and metrics (e.g. via OpenTelemetry)
to track agent decisions and performance
○ Environment: The “perception” layer – how agents receive input (user
requests, sensor data, events) and output results (UI, API responses).

slides.title: Agent Patterns
slides.raw_text:
Agent Patterns
● Concurrent Pattern: Multiple agents work on the same input in parallel.
● Group-chat Pattern: Agents and optional humans interact via chat interface.
● Handoff Pattern: Agents dynamically pass the control
● Magentic Pattern: A central manager selects which special agent to invoke
next
● Collector Pattern

slides.title: Agent patterns contd.,
slides.raw_text:
Agent patterns contd.,
Planner–Executor: Planner creates a plan and executor performs
1
steps. A type of Hand-off pattern.
Reflex Agents: Respond to stimuli, they dont have memory. Used in
chatbots, simple automation.
Hierarchical Agents: High-level agent delegates tasks. This is also a
type of magentic pattern.
Autonomous Loop Agents:They have Continuous sense–think–act
cycle
